questionText: The Global Goals were adopted by all United Nations member states in ___ as a universal call to action to end poverty, protect the planet and ensure that all people enjoy peace and prosperity by ___.
Which of the following is true?
options: The SDGs are a bold commitment to solely increase the GDP of India
Dealing with climate change has no impact on gender equality
Success in one SDG affects success of the others
Only 14 out of the 17 SDGs are interconnected

questionText: In June 1992, at the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, more than 178 countries adopted ___.
Brundtland Report was released in 1987 by the WCED. WCED stands for ___.
options: World Commission on Environment and Development

questionText: Extreme poverty means living on less than ___ a day.
___ poverty measures reveal who is poor and how they are poor.
SDG 2 aims end all forms of ___ by 2030.
Which of the following is not one of the indicators of the Global Hunger Index?
options: prevalence of wasting and stunting in children under 5 years
proportion of undernourished in the population
proportion of obese people in the population

questionText: For rural areas, a minimum service delivery of 55 lpcd has been fixed under___.
Forests cover about ___ percent of the Earth’s surface.
Which of the following is a characteristic of ESD as described in the framework?
options: It promotes learning only for children
It uses only two pedagogical techniques

questionText: The Declaration on the Right to Development (1986) established that everyone is entitled to participate in and enjoy:
options: economic, political, psychological and communist development
economic, social, cultural and political development

questionText: Text based methods for assessing ESD programs can be improved by using ___.
SDGS are for the following except ___.
Policies like employment and labor rights, care and family leves, and financial regulation are often called the ___ policies.
Which of the following is involved in the development of the HRE indicator framework?
options: The Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights

questionText: ___ plays a role in peace education.
The enterprise of education at its most profound level is ___.
UNESCO’s World in 2030 survey revealed that respondents see ___ as one of the top global challenges.
Education for sustainable development and peace develops people’s skills to take actions that improve our quality of life __________.
options: for now and for the future

questionText: Global citizenship is a belief that individuals are members of ___ networks.
A global citizen ___.
options: is not aware of the wider world
is confused about their place in the world
takes an active role in their community
do not care much about the planet

questionText: Global citizenship education is based on ___ domains of learning.
___ is UNESCO’s first Category 1 education-related institute in the Asia-Pacific region.

SEL stands for________.
Which of the following is a role taken by a teacher in ESD?

Which of the following statements is not true?
options: green jobs contribute to preserving environmental quality
green jobs are only found in the agriculture sector
green jobs must be accessible to all

questionText: GESO stands for ___.
The classification of occupations into GIDO, GESO and GNEO is given by US National Center for ___ .

Industry-institute interaction is an indicator of ___.
Sustainable entrepreneurship includes ___.
ESD requires ___ learning methods.
Which of the following is a transversal competency?
___ is the ability to create good relationships and adapt to different social situations.
Sustainable health is ___.
options: a balance of mind, body and spirit

questionText: Siddha comes under the ___.
Which of the following is not a problem of healthcare infrastructure in India?
Which of the following is a health system building block as suggested by WHO?

A ___ is an environment in which data of medical interest can be stored and made easily available.
The POSHAN Abhiyan in India aims to improve nutritional outcomes for ___.
Sus-QI stands for ___.
Which of the following is not a stage of Sus-QI?
Food insecurity is measured on the ___.
The UN lists ___ levels of food security for any given country.
Poor infrastructure can cause hunger in a country.
In high income countries, hunger is mainly caused due to poverty that results from ___.
Which of the following is true about hunger?
options: It is a cause of war
It is an effect of war
It is both a cause and effect of war
It is neither a cause nor an effect of war

questionText: ___ are most vulnerable to the harmful effects of hidden hunger.
Desertification is typically a result of ___.
Which of the following is a key initiative in India for the problem of hunger?
In the past, MGNREGA has ___.
options: reduced distress migration from rural areas

questionText: Wheat productivity can suffer if ___.
Bringing forgotten and neglected foods like yam into cultivation is called ___.
Home gardening has been proven as a reliable source to supplement food and nutritional security at the household level.
Which of the following is a type of energy?
In IPCC by UN, I stands for ___.

The greatest contributor to air pollution in China is ___.
In Japan, a city set a target for ___ by 2022 after the 2011 disaster.

The Paris Agreement is a legally binding treaty on ___ adopted at COP 21.

Stubble burning usually takes place in the months of ___.
Stubble burning is ___.
The Okayama Commitment is from ___.
Which of the following is a policy support mechanism of Okayama Commitment?
Education is related to SDG 9 such that ___.
options: education helps in skills required to build resilient infrastructure
education enhances skills to use natural resources more sustainably
education is critical to lifting people out of poverty

questionText: The frame model structures sustainability competencies into ___ goal dimensions.
Sustainability issues must be looked at through a ___ approach.
Technology and Science can alone solve all our sustainability problems.
GAP in the context of ESD stands for ___.
___ competency is the ability to understand and evaluate multiple futures.

___ intelligences is proposed by Howard Gardner.
___ intelligence refers to using one’s body in skilled ways.
Big transformation implies changes in individual action are intertwined with ___.
In ___ learning, learners start to question all the things they knew.
Which of the following is a key principle of adult education?
___ Sustainable Development is a process through which people share control over development initiatives.
___ is the common regulatory body for higher education in India.
Sustainable leaders look at immediate, short-term gains.
The need of the hour is to have leaders who ___.
options: focus only on building technical solutions

questionText: Which of the following is a part of the 3Ps of triple bottom line?

The sustainability mindset involves ___.

__leadership is also called missionary leadership.

___ India gives a theory U (co-initiating, co-sensing, etc.).

options: willingness to stay in comfort zone

questionText: According to Maxwell, there are ___ levels of leadership.
The ___ program describes global leaders as dynamic, engaged and driven.
SDG ___ is to make cities and human settlements inclusive, safe, resilient, and sustainable.
___resources are those that exist in the absence of human intervention.

options: how long will I use it?

questionText: A bulb thrown out after usage forms __.
SDG ___ seeks to promote international trade and equitable trading system.
